---
title: "Lectures"
author: "Author: Matthew J. Cossley"
date: "Last update: `r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: false
    toc_float:
        collapsed: true
        smooth_scroll: true
    toc_depth: 4
    fig_caption: yes
    # code_folding: show
    number_sections: false
    theme: cosmo
fontsize: 14pt
---

## Course Intro
##### [Lecture 1: Course intro, `Python`, math](lecture_1.html)

## Neuron models
##### [Lecture 2: Simple Models](lecture_2.html)
##### [Lecture 3: Hodgkin–Huxley](lecture_3.html)

## Modelling postsynaptic potentials
##### [Lecture 4: postsynaptic potentials](lecture_4.html)

## Building spiking networks
##### [Lecture 5: Spiking networks](lecture_5.html)

## Reinforcement Learning (RL)
##### [Lecture 6: Temporal-difference RL](lecture_6.html)
##### [Lecture 7: Q-learning](lecture_7.html)
##### Lecture 8: Model-based RL

## Linear dynamical systems
##### Lecture 9: State-space models of sensorimotor learning

## Deep neural network (DNN) models of the visual system
##### Lecture 10: Early deep models (HMAX)
##### Lecture 11: Modern DNNs

## Parameter estimation and model selection
##### Lecture 12: Least squares and maximum likelihood
##### Lecture 13: Differential evolution
##### Lecture 14: TBA
